title: Job Vacancies at TeKnowledge
content:
At TeKnowledge, we turn complexity into clarity – and potential into progress. We go beyond problem-solving to transform how you grow. By blending cutting-edge technology with expert insight, we help enterprises, governments and tech vendors tackle their biggest challenges while preparing them for what’s next. From strategy to execution, we drive smarter, faster, and more sustained innovation—empowering organizations with the capability and control to lead with confidence.

We are recruiting to fill the following positions below:

1.) AI Enabled Quality Engineer

Job Location: Lagos

Overview
     

            
    




	We are building an AI-Native Engineering team and are looking for Quality Engineers who use modern AI tools as a core part of how they design, execute, and automate testing.
	Working as part of a team across multiple projects simultaneously, you will provide quality assurance support to designated software, organise and track incident response activities, and simplify audit and compliance work.
	You will collaborate closely with product managers, architects, and developers across the full testing spectrum — database, unit, API, security, performance and UAT — to ensure that code is production-ready before it ships.
	The ideal candidate is detail-oriented, automation-minded, and proactive in adopting tools that improve test coverage and speed. If this describes you, we would like to hear from you.


Responsibilities


	Provide quality assurance support to designated software across multiple concurrent projects
	Perform feature acceptance, regression, and UI testing
	Design, maintain, and execute test cases across database, unit, API, security, and UAT testing
	Build and maintain test automation across these testing layers
	Analyse design documentation and production requirements to define effective test strategies
	Create and maintain comprehensive test documentation
	Organise and track incident response activities
	Support audit and compliance activities, simplifying processes where possible
	Participate actively in the release process and ensure code is production-ready before deployment
	Collaborate closely with product managers, architects, and developers throughout the SDLC
	AI-Native Quality Engineering.


AI-Native Quality Engineering:


	Apply AI tools (e.g., Claude, GitHub Copilot, Cursor) to generate test cases, test data, and documentation
	Use prompt engineering techniques to accelerate test design and edge-case identification
	Build AI-assisted automation across database, unit, API, security, and UAT testing
	Use AI tools to identify defects, vulnerabilities, and performance issues earlier in the cycle
	Critically evaluate AI-generated test artefacts and validate their correctness before use
	Contribute to internal AI-assisted testing frameworks and reusable patterns


Qualifications
Experience:


	Minimum of 2 years of proven experience in a similar Quality Engineering or Software Testing role
	Demonstrated experience in test case design, maintenance, and execution
	Good theoretical knowledge of QA and software testing principles and the software development process
	Specialisation in Computer Science or Software Engineering
	Intermediate English, both written and oral.


Technical Skills:


	Experience with API testing
	Experience with test automation
	Familiarity with testing across multiple layers (database, unit, API, security, UAT)
	Experience using a test management tool
	AI & Automation Skills
	Familiarity with AI-assisted testing or coding tools (e.g., Claude, Copilot, Cursor)
	Ability to validate and refine AI-generated test artefacts.


Preferred Qualifications:


	Experience with performance testing
	Experience with security testing
	Working knowledge of SQL or relational databases for database testing
	Familiarity with prompt engineering for test case generation
	Experience contributing to continuous testing pipelines.


Click here to apply online

 

2.) Mobile AI Engineer

Job Location: Victoria Island, Lagos

Responsibilities


	Develop a mobile app using Flutter or React Native.
	Use AI tools (Claude Code / Claude Design) to generate application logic and UI.
	Design and validate user flows for:
	
		Onboarding
		KYC
		Transactions
	
	
	Integrate mobile applications with backend services and APIs.
	Ensure performance, stability, and production readiness.


Qualifications


	5 to 9 years.
	Technical education.
	Bachelor’s degree (required).
	Technical degree (required).
	Master’s degree (preferred).
	High school (required).
	Strong experience in Flutter or React Native (mandatory).
	Experience building production mobile applications.
	Strong understanding of:
	
		API integrations
		Mobile architecture
	
	
	Experience using AI coding tools (Claude, Copilot, GPT).
	Experience with financial applications such as lending, payments, or wallets.
	Experience with mobile onboarding flows and transaction UX.
	Understanding of secure and regulated mobile environments.
	Experience with hybrid mobile development using Flutter or React Native.
	Experience integrating mobile applications with backend APIs.
	Familiarity with Azure cloud environments.
	Experience with AI development tools such as Claude Code and Claude Design.
	Product-oriented engineering mindset.
	Strong UX validation capability.
	Comfortable with fast, AI-driven delivery.
	English: Professional.
	Local Language: Professional.


Click here to apply online

 

3.) Backend AI Engineer (Financial Services Platform)

Req ID: 17527
Job Location: Victoria Island, Lagos

Responsibilities


	Build backend services and APIs on top of existing core systems.
	Develop systems using AI-assisted coding tools, including Claude Code.
	Design and implement:
	
		Service layers above core banking systems.
		Integration services connecting internal and external platforms.
	
	
	Manage data flows and system interactions across multiple services.
	Ensure system scalability, performance, reliability, and security.
	Develop and maintain APIs and microservices-based solutions.
	Design and implement event-driven and messaging-based integrations.
	Collaborate with cross-functional teams to deliver high-quality platform capabilities.
	Validate system designs and solutions through testing and continuous improvement.


Qualifications


	Experience: 5–9 years


Education Qualifications:


	Bachelor's degree required.
	Technical degree required.
	Master's degree preferred.
	High school diploma required.


Technical Skills:


	Strong backend development experience with .NET, Node.js, and/or Python.
	Strong experience with:
	APIs and microservices.
	Distributed systems.
	Experience with cloud platforms, preferably Microsoft Azure.
	Experience using AI tools for software development.


Platform & Architecture Skills (Mandatory):


	Experience or strong understanding of:
	Operational Data Stores (ODS).
	Event-driven architecture.
	Messaging systems (queues, pub/sub).
	Document handling and storage.
	Identity and Access Management (IAM).


Domain Experience;


	Financial services systems, including lending, payments, and fintech platforms.
	Transaction processing systems.
	Integrations with external services such as AML, KYC, and PSP providers.


Technology Environment:


	Cloud: Azure.
	Architecture:
	
		APIs and microservices.
		Event-driven systems.
		Messaging-based integrations.
	
	


Languages:


	Professional proficiency in English.
	Professional proficiency in the local language.


Click here to apply online    

    

        Click here to Apply

Internal Auditor Job at Quicky Chillis Restaurant




        
    

        
        Quicky Chillis is a 24-hour restaurant in Adewale bus stop, Governors Road Ikotun, Lagos State.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Internal Auditor

Job Location: Ikotun, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilities
     

            
    




	Examine financial records: Check books, invoices, receipts, bank statements for accuracy
	Verify compliance: Make sure the company follows accounting standards, tax laws, and internal policies
	Test internal controls: See if systems in place actually prevent fraud/errors
	Detect misstatements: Look for errors, fraud, or anything that could mislead stakeholders
	Issue audit report: Give an opinionon the financial statements.


Salary
N120,000 - N150,000 Monthly

Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

title: Job Openings at Rand Merchant Bank (RMB)
content:
Rand Merchant Bank (RMB), a division of FirstRand Bank Limited, is a leading African corporate and investment bank and part of one of the largest financial services groups in Africa. We offer our clients innovative, value-added advisory, funding, trading, corporate banking and principal investing solutions.

We are recruiting to fill the following positions below:

1.) Internal Auditor

Job Location: Lagos
Job Type: Full Time

Job Description
     

            
    




	This role reports directly to the Chief Audit Executive of RMB Nigeria. 
	The IT Auditor will ensure the adequacy and effectiveness of risk management and control over IT Systems and Platforms, General IT Controls, CAATs, and Projects. 
	Furthermore, to take accountability for the management and development of the IT audit skills and to provide consulting services to the clients. 
	IT audits cover the following subject matters: IT and cybersecurity reviews, IT service providers reviews, Business Continuity Management and Disaster Recovery, Project Management, Pre and Post implementation reviews for IT projects, Change Management, Logical Access Management, IT Governance, Data Backup, Cloud Computing, IT Operations, and reviews for Industry/Regulatory compliance (SWIFT/PCI-DSS).


Responsibilities
The successful candidate will be responsible for:


	Delivering Assurance and Consulting Services.
	To provide assurance on the adequacy and effectiveness of risk management and controls over IT systems and platforms, general IT controls, data analysis and projects.
	Ability to work collaboratively and identify opportunities and driving integrated audit approaches between process audit and IT audit according to timeliness agreed with the audit committee, etc.
	Staying abreast of emerging technologies, industry trends and regulatory requirements impacting IT audit practices.
	Evaluate and conduct reviews on a range of Hardware, Operating Systems, Databases, Network Infrastructure and applications.
	Perform control reviews on systems development, operations, programming, control, and security procedures and standards.
	Review system backup, disaster recovery and maintenance procedures.
	Ensuring audits are completed in accordance with the Group Internal Audit methodology and audit standards as prescribed by Institute of Internal Auditors.
	Perform individual audits in respect of the risks and controls on the process being audited, and advising on technical, methodology and statutory issues.
	Discussion of the draft report with the auditee, finalization of management comments, action plans and target dates for implementation.
	Facilitating dispute resolution with line management, if required.
	Work closely with the RMB Nigeria Group Internal audit team and the Group and Brader Africa IT auditors. 
	Expressing an audit opinion over the acceptability of the residual risk in the process under review.
	Drive the relationships with the customer base and ensure that customers are provided with world-class perspectives and solutions.
	Conduct process audit as assigned by the Chief Audit Executive from time to time.
	Carry out other duties and investigations as directed by the Chief Audit Executive. 


Experience & Qualifications


	Bachelor’s Degree in information systems or related field
	4 years of experience in IT audit, process audit, risk management, or technology assurance.
	Professional certifications: ACCA, CISA; CRISC, CISSP, ISO 27001 Lead Auditor (advantageous)
	Extensive experience and knowledge on General Controls Review, Application Controls Review, CAATS and Project Auditing is a pre-requisite.
	Strong understanding of IT governance frameworks, compliance standards, regulatory frameworks and cybersecurity principles.
	Proficiency in audit analytics tools, power BI, and Microsoft Office suite.


General Competencies: 


	Customer Service Orientation
	Analysis and attention to detail
	Time and Self- Management
	Drive and results orientation
	Verbal and written communication
	Leading and Supervising.


Application Closing Date: 6th July, 2026.

Click here to apply online

 

 

2.) Corporate Coverage Analyst

Job Location: Lagos
Job Type: Full Time

Job Description


	The candidate will support Relationship Managers in delivering client-focused solutions, while also engaging internal teams to drive product awareness, sales effectiveness, and seamless client execution. 
	The role provides exposure to the full value chain of Corporate and Investment Banking, supporting both client retention and new business origination.


Responsibilities
The successful candidate will be responsible for:

Client Coverage & Business Support:


	Support Relationship Managers in managing client portfolios, including preparation of client briefs, reports, and presentations.
	Conduct client and sector research, including analysis of financial statements and market trends, to support business development initiatives.
	Assist in identifying, onboarding, and retaining clients in line with coverage strategy. 
	Maintain and update opportunity tracking tools and pipeline reporting.


Product & Sales Enablement:


	Engage with internal stakeholders (e.g., branch teams, product partners, and contact centre teams where relevant) to understand client needs and drive tailored solutions.
	Support product awareness initiatives through training, presentations, and knowledge-sharing sessions across the business.
	Maintain a strong understanding of RMB products, processes, and fulfilment requirements to support deal execution.
	Contribute to the achievement of sales targets through effective coordination and client support.


Client Experience & Stakeholder Management:


	Build and maintain strong internal networks across business units to ensure seamless client delivery.
	Support the coordination of client engagements, ensuring consistency and high-quality service delivery.
	Provide insights on client trends and market developments to enhance client conversations and relationship management.


Market Intelligence & Continuous Improvement:


	Maintain up-to-date knowledge of industry, regulatory, and market developments to inform strategic decisions.
	Identify opportunities to improve internal processes, client service delivery, and sales effectiveness.
	Support reporting and business reviews with accurate data and insights.


Experience & Qualifications


	Bachelor’s Degree in Finance, Economics, Accounting, or a related discipline.
	3–6 years of experience in corporate banking, investment banking, or financial services. 
	Relevant professional certification (e.g., CFA, ACCA) is an added advantage.
	Exposure to client coverage, product support, or sales enablement roles.


Technical Skills: 


	Strong financial analysis and research capability.
	Good understanding of corporate and investment banking products.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office tools.


General Competencies: 


	Client-centric mindset.
	Strong communication and presentation skills.
	Commercial awareness and business acumen.
	Stakeholder management and collaboration.
	Attention to detail and strong execution capability.
	Proactive, self-motivated, and adaptable.


Application Closing Date: 6th July, 2026.

Click here to apply online

 

3.) Debt Financing Solutions Senior Transactor

Job Requisition ID: R50641
Job Location: Lagos, Nigeria.
Job Schedule: Full time

Job Description


	RMB Nigeria is looking to appoint a senior transactor within DFS. The individual must be an entrepreneurial self-starter who has the energy and passion to grow RMB’s IBD business in Nigeria.
	The successful candidate will also be required to provide corporate term lending and DCM solutions to clients based in West Africa (primarily Nigeria and Ghana).
	Key focus clients include Sovereigns, State Owned Enterprises, Supranational, Financial Institutions, Multinational, and large local indigenous corporates.


Responsibilities
The successful candidate will be responsible for:


	Lead the origination, structuring, and implementation process around bespoke term lending solutions. The candidate will be able to leverage off the coverage and existing DFS staff, credit teams, IBD in-country head within RMBN as well as the product skills based within DFS in South Africa and London
	Have an understanding of credit rating advisory and listing requirements that may be necessary to facilitate the issuance of bonds within Nigeria and Ghana
	Focus on developing strategic relationships with a target list of strategic names that have been identified as key to the RMBN and DFS strategy (this list of clients will be provided in the interview process), together with designated Coverage personnel
	Interact successfully with the in-country and South African credit team, in-country Global Markets and Treasury & Trade Solutions teams, as well as the rest of the IBD Broader African team
	Close transactions by obtaining the relevant internal approvals for the transaction and leading or actively participating in the successful conclusion of negotiations
	Add value to transactions for the benefit of RMB and/or the client through knowledge of credit risk
	Enhance the bankability of transactions by identifying and mitigating key risk issues
	Implement transactions by preparing or managing the preparation of complex multiple jurisdiction documents and compliance with all required procedures, laws, and regulations
	Monitoring & management of deals post-closure


Other:


	Mentoring and coaching of junior Transactors
	Cross-selling of bank products to its clients
	Internal stakeholder relationship management


Experience & Qualifications


	At least 5 years of deal-making experience (corporate & structured finance)
	Prior relevant deal experience.  Deal experience in Nigeria will be advantageous
	Relevant bachelor’s degree in relevant subject; ACCA, CFA or MBA advantageous


General Competencies:


	The successful candidate should have excellent communication and interpersonal skills in order to form lasting client relationships.
	S/he must be self–motivated and able to work well both in a team as well as independently.
	S/he must have the ability to apply knowledge and experience in a creative manner to add value to the department.
	S/he must maintain the highest degree of professionalism and business acumen.
	The successful candidate must be happy to travel both in West Africa and to South Africa (and internationally as required).
	Strong relationship-building skills, with the ability to fulfil a consultation profile to advise borrowers on processes and structures
	Sound verbal and written communication skills
	Ability to negotiate and persuade internal and external stakeholders
	Sound analytical skills
	Strong numerical reasoning skills
	The ability to plan and write reports
	Attention to detail and a passion for accuracy
	Ability to work well in a team environment
	Sound presentation skills
	Ability to identify key drivers and opportunities to add to the increased Return on Investment for RMB
	Knowledge of specialist sector skills
	Assertive
	Good level of resilience
	Sound administrative skills
	Technical Prerequisites:
	Understanding and knowledge of all financial instruments
	Broad knowledge of Tax, Law and Accounting (an understanding of the taxation of companies, financial institutions and shareholders will be advantageous).
	Deep understanding of credit analysis and debt structuring.
	A working knowledge of the law of contracts. 
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite


Application Closing Date: 2nd July, 2026; 22:55

Click here to apply online

     

    

        Click here to Apply

Internal Audit Officer Job at Coleman Technical Industries Limited




        
    

        
        Coleman Technical Industries Limited, the manufacturer of the reputable Coleman Wires and Cables, is Sub-the Saharan Africa’s largest cable manufacturer, renowned for quality, reliability, and innovation. From its ultra-modern manufacturing facilities in Arepo and Sagamu, including its recently commissioned state-of-the-art plant, producing the best fibre-optic cables with FRP in Africa, the company remains committed to industrial excellence, safety, and national developmen

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Internal Audit Officer

Job Location: Arepo, Ogun
Employment Type: Full-time

Key Responsibilities
     

            
    




	Ensure and monitor store compliance to Company policies, procedures and systems
	Report variance and ensure proper follow up
	Monitor the implementation of internal audit recommendations to ensure the satisfactory resolution of identified significant issues
	Conduct OSC – Operations Standard Compliance on stores to ensure compliance to store standards
	Identify loopholes and recommend risk aversion measures and cost savings.
	Ensure follow up on gaps identified and communicate to Line Manager
	Perform and control the full audit cycle including risk management and control management over operations’ effectiveness, financial reliability and compliance with all applicable directives and regulations.
	Proactively identify trends and issues from audit findings and formulate recommendations for improving internal controls and addressing identified weaknesses/gaps across all locations/markets in which the company operates
	Review and report on the accuracy, timeliness and relevance of the financial and other information provided to management for decision making
	Monitor the implementation of internal audit recommendations to ensure the satisfactory resolution of identified significant issues
	Report on stores compliance to standards
	Develop and maintain the company’s Quality Management system, ensuring full compliance with its guidelines and a consolidated uniform approach across the company
	Assist management in the identification of business improvement opportunities in line with the recommendations of the Quality Policy and provide required assistance in managing projects which are spin-offs of such exercises


Job Requirements


	Bachelor’s Degree in Accounting or Finance.
	Minimum of Two(2) years of experience as an Internal Auditor.
	Relevant professional qualifications from recognised professional bodies such as ICAN, ACCA/ACA.
	Proven knowledge of auditing standards and procedures, laws, rules and regulations.
	Proficiency in accounting software and spreadsheets.]


Desired Personal Attributes:


	Integrity
	Proactive self-starter
	Results Orientation
	Strategic Thought Process and Follow through
	Assertiveness & tenacity
	Ability to work with all levels of management, build partnerships and teams
	Highly organized and significant ability to multi-task effectively
	Ability to cope with and work under pressure.


Method of Application:
Interested and qualified candidates should send their resume to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Senior Internal Audit Officer",
  "description": "Coleman Technical Industries Limited, the manufacturer of the reputable Coleman Wires and Cables, is Sub-the Saharan Africa&rsquo;s largest cable manufacturer, renowned for quality, reliability, and innovation. From its ultra-modern manufacturing facilities in Arepo and Sagamu, including its recently commissioned state-of-the-art plant, producing the best fibre-optic cables with FRP in Africa, the company remains committed to industrial excellence, safety, and national developmen

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Senior Internal Audit Officer

Job Location: APO, Abuja (FCT)
Employment Type: Full-time

Key Responsibilities


	Participate in the identification and evaluation of business risks associated with strategic and operational objectives of the organisation.
	Evaluate the adequacy of process design and the effectiveness of controls in meeting business and control objectives
	Execute developed test plans for each engagement to achieve set audit objectives and address risk exposures within the organisation.
	Ensure proper documentation of audit work papers, findings and recommendations in accordance with the methodology
	Assisting with engagement administration including preparation of project plans, budgets and minutes of meetings
	To participate and prepare reports in the periodic and annual stock count exercise and crude fiscalization exercise and ensure that the activities are carried out in line with the company&#39;s procedures
	Provide on the job training to internal audit officers on assigned tasks
	Performs review of work done by internal audit officers
	Participate in the execution of audits and ensure efficient and effective execution of annual audit plan
	Ensure delivery of assigned audit tasks in a timely manner
	Ensure compliance with audit methodology and provide input to the development of audit work programs
	Perform procedures to assess compliance with the laid down policies, processes and procedures
	Review systems and procedures across all business areas and recommend improvements to existing processes and procedures, where appropriate
	Participate in investigations of control breaches, as may be necessary from time to time
	Perform other activities as assigned by Superiors


Key Performance Indicators


	Quality of audit documentation and work papaers
	Timeliness of audit deliverables


Job Requirements


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Accounting or Finance.
	Minimum of Five (5) years of experience as a Senior Internal Auditor in a structured business environment.
	Relevant professional qualifications from recognised professional bodies such as ICAN, ACCA/ACA.
	Proven knowledge of auditing standards and procedures, laws, rules and regulations.
	Proficiency in accounting software and spreadsheets.


Desired Personal Attributes:


	Integrity
	Proactive self-starter
	Results Orientation
	Strategic Thought Process and Follow through
	Assertiveness &amp; tenacity
	Ability to work with all levels of management, build partnerships and teams
	Highly organized and significant ability to multi-task effectively
	Ability to cope with and work under pressure.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their resume to: careers@colemancables.com using &quot;Senior Internal Audit Officer&quot; as the subject of the email.",

Senior Internal Audit Officer Job at Coleman Technical Industries Limited




        
    

        
        Coleman Technical Industries Limited, the manufacturer of the reputable Coleman Wires and Cables, is Sub-the Saharan Africa’s largest cable manufacturer, renowned for quality, reliability, and innovation. From its ultra-modern manufacturing facilities in Arepo and Sagamu, including its recently commissioned state-of-the-art plant, producing the best fibre-optic cables with FRP in Africa, the company remains committed to industrial excellence, safety, and national developmen

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Senior Internal Audit Officer

Job Location: APO, Abuja (FCT)
Employment Type: Full-time

Key Responsibilities
     

            
    




	Participate in the identification and evaluation of business risks associated with strategic and operational objectives of the organisation.
	Evaluate the adequacy of process design and the effectiveness of controls in meeting business and control objectives
	Execute developed test plans for each engagement to achieve set audit objectives and address risk exposures within the organisation.
	Ensure proper documentation of audit work papers, findings and recommendations in accordance with the methodology
	Assisting with engagement administration including preparation of project plans, budgets and minutes of meetings
	To participate and prepare reports in the periodic and annual stock count exercise and crude fiscalization exercise and ensure that the activities are carried out in line with the company's procedures
	Provide on the job training to internal audit officers on assigned tasks
	Performs review of work done by internal audit officers
	Participate in the execution of audits and ensure efficient and effective execution of annual audit plan
	Ensure delivery of assigned audit tasks in a timely manner
	Ensure compliance with audit methodology and provide input to the development of audit work programs
	Perform procedures to assess compliance with the laid down policies, processes and procedures
	Review systems and procedures across all business areas and recommend improvements to existing processes and procedures, where appropriate
	Participate in investigations of control breaches, as may be necessary from time to time
	Perform other activities as assigned by Superiors


Key Performance Indicators


	Quality of audit documentation and work papaers
	Timeliness of audit deliverables


Job Requirements


	Bachelor’s Degree in Accounting or Finance.
	Minimum of Five (5) years of experience as a Senior Internal Auditor in a structured business environment.
	Relevant professional qualifications from recognised professional bodies such as ICAN, ACCA/ACA.
	Proven knowledge of auditing standards and procedures, laws, rules and regulations.
	Proficiency in accounting software and spreadsheets.


Desired Personal Attributes:


	Integrity
	Proactive self-starter
	Results Orientation
	Strategic Thought Process and Follow through
	Assertiveness & tenacity
	Ability to work with all levels of management, build partnerships and teams
	Highly organized and significant ability to multi-task effectively
	Ability to cope with and work under pressure.


Method of Application
